faq.question: What time is it in Cairo right now?
faq.answer: Cairo operates on Eastern European Time (EET/UTC+2). The city does not currently observe daylight saving time, maintaining UTC+2 year-round since Egypt discontinued DST in 2015. Cairo is 7 hours ahead of New York (EST) and 2 hours ahead of London (GMT).

faq.question: What time zone is Cairo in?
faq.answer: Cairo uses Eastern European Time (EET), which is UTC+2. Egypt permanently adopted UTC+2 in 2015 after discontinuing seasonal clock changes. This timezone is shared with several African and Middle Eastern cities including Johannesburg, Athens, and Jerusalem.

faq.question: Does Cairo observe daylight saving time?
faq.answer: No. Egypt discontinued daylight saving time in 2015 and now maintains Eastern European Time (UTC+2) year-round. Prior to 2015, Cairo observed daylight saving time from late April to late September, shifting to UTC+3 during summer months.

faq.question: What is the time difference between Cairo and New York?
faq.answer: Cairo is 7 hours ahead of New York during Eastern Standard Time (EST) and 6 hours ahead during Eastern Daylight Time (EDT). When it's 12:00 PM in New York, it's 7:00 PM in Cairo during winter months and 6:00 PM during summer months when the US observes DST.

faq.question: What is the time difference between Cairo and London?
faq.answer: Cairo is 2 hours ahead of London during Greenwich Mean Time (GMT) and 1 hour ahead during British Summer Time (BST). When it's 9:00 AM in London, it's 11:00 AM in Cairo during winter and 10:00 AM during British summer months.

faq.question: What time does the stock exchange open in Cairo?
faq.answer: The Egyptian Exchange (EGX) in Cairo operates from 10:00 AM to 2:30 PM EET (UTC+2), Sunday through Thursday. Trading is closed on Fridays and Saturdays, aligning with Egypt's weekend. The exchange also closes for Islamic holidays including Eid al-Fitr and Eid al-Adha.

faq.question: What is the best time to call Cairo from the US?
faq.answer: The optimal calling window from the US East Coast is 8:00 AM to 11:00 AM EST (3:00 PM to 6:00 PM Cairo time), catching Cairo's late afternoon business hours. From the West Coast, call between 5:00 AM and 8:00 AM PST to reach Cairo during working hours before businesses close.

faq.question: What are typical business hours in Cairo?
faq.answer: Cairo business hours typically run from 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM EET, Sunday through Thursday. Many government offices operate 8:00 AM to 3:00 PM. The Egyptian workweek is Sunday to Thursday, with Friday and Saturday as the weekend. During Ramadan, hours shift significantly with many businesses opening later and closing earlier.

seo_description: Current time in Cairo, Egypt (UTC+2). Live clock, timezone info, business hours, and time differences with major cities worldwide. No DST since 2015.
seo_title: Current Time in Cairo, Egypt — EET Timezone & World Clock (UTC+2)

content_blocks.title: Cairo Time Zone Guide
content_blocks.content: Cairo operates on Eastern European Time (EET/UTC+2) year-round, maintaining a consistent timezone since Egypt permanently discontinued daylight saving time in 2015. As Africa's largest metropolitan area and the Arab world's cultural capital, Cairo's timezone positioning makes it a strategic hub for business across three continents—with morning overlap with European markets and afternoon synchronization with Gulf states. The city's UTC+2 offset places it 7 hours ahead of New York during EST and 2 hours ahead of London during GMT, creating natural business windows for international coordination. Compare Cairo's time with New York, London, or Dubai to plan your international calls and meetings. Egypt's decision to eliminate seasonal clock changes in 2015 simplified scheduling for the nation's growing tech sector and tourism industry, which serves over 13 million annual visitors. The stable UTC+2 timezone also benefits Cairo's role as a filming location for international productions and its position as a regional financial center hosting the Egyptian Exchange.

content_blocks.title: Business Hours and Working Week in Cairo
content_blocks.content: Cairo follows a Sunday-to-Thursday working week, with Friday and Saturday comprising the Egyptian weekend—a schedule that aligns with most Middle Eastern and North African nations but differs from Western business calendars. Standard business hours run from 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM EET, though government offices typically operate 8:00 AM to 3:00 PM. Banks generally open 9:00 AM to 2:00 PM Sunday through Thursday. The Egyptian Exchange (EGX) trades from 10:00 AM to 2:30 PM, offering a 4.5-hour window that overlaps with late-morning European markets. International businesses in Cairo often maintain extended hours to accommodate Western timezone coordination, with many multinational offices scheduling calls between 3:00 PM and 6:00 PM EET to catch morning hours in US Eastern time. During the holy month of Ramadan, business hours shift dramatically—most organizations operate 10:00 AM to 4:00 PM with reduced productivity, as employees fast from dawn to sunset. Use our meeting planner tool to find optimal collaboration times between Cairo and your location, accounting for the unique Sunday-start workweek.

content_blocks.title: International Calling and Communication with Cairo
content_blocks.content: Reaching Cairo from international locations requires strategic timing due to the 7-hour gap with US East Coast and 2-hour difference with UK during standard time. For US-based callers on Eastern time, the prime window is 8:00 AM to 11:00 AM EST (3:00 PM to 6:00 PM Cairo time), catching the final hours of Cairo's business day when decision-makers are still accessible. West Coast callers should aim for 5:00 AM to 8:00 AM PST to reach Cairo's mid-afternoon. European callers have more flexibility—London businesses can connect with Cairo from 7:00 AM to 3:00 PM GMT, covering Cairo's entire 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM workday. Remember that Cairo's weekend falls on Friday-Saturday, so scheduling Thursday calls after 2:00 PM EET risks catching staff in early weekend mode. The city's position at UTC+2 also creates natural synchronization with Gulf states like Dubai (UTC+4) and Riyadh (UTC+3), making afternoon meetings ideal for pan-regional coordination. Check the exact time difference with London or Tokyo before scheduling. Egypt's country code is +20, with Cairo's area code being 2. Mobile penetration exceeds 95%, and WhatsApp is the dominant business communication platform alongside email, with many professionals responding faster to WhatsApp messages during working hours.
